reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASS - ReceiveBlock is actively called from P2P sync layer (subscriber_beacon_blocks.go:42). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASS - Violation occurs when processing untrusted P2P blocks. Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASS - Code reading is factually correct. At receive_block.go:516-517, deposit validation errors wrapped in execReqErr are returned as raw errors instead of invalidBlock{error: err} (L519). The P2P handler at subscriber_beacon_blocks.go:43 checks blockchain.IsInvalidBlock(err) to mark bad blocks; execReqErr errors bypass this check, so invalid blocks with deposit validation failures are not properly blacklisted in fork choice. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASS - Attacker-triggered via P2P. An attacker can submit a P2P block with nil or malformed deposit request data. The validation error at transition_no_verify_sig.go:95 wraps deposit processing errors in execReqErr, which is detected at receive_block.go:516 and returned raw, bypassing the invalidBlock wrapper. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): Property text from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W4B5_1771748579.json:379-395 states 'Every deposit validation failure must terminate with an InvalidBlock exception' with assertion 'forall deposit_data: !valid(deposit_data) => raises(InvalidBlock)'. The code violates this by returning execReqErr instead. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASS - Deposit validation is Fusaka-specific (EIP-7685 execution requests), in-scope per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending a block with invalid deposit requests (wrong offsets, wrong sizes, nil data, etc.), causing the validation to fail at ProcessDepositRequests, which returns execReqErr. This error propagates to receive_block.go:516, bypasses invalidBlock wrapping, and is returned to the P2P handler. The block is not marked bad, potentially allowing propagation or incorrect fork choice behavior. Severity: Single-client bug (Prysm at 31% CL share) affecting deposit validation. Impact capped at High per client_diversity threshold (>33% network). Concrete attack: attacker submits malformed P2P block, bypasses block invalidation, potential for consensus divergence.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Every deposit validation failure must terminate with an InvalidBlock exception. (forall deposit_data: !valid(deposit_data) => raises(InvalidBlock))
